programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom. Hyacinth arranges one of her candlelight suppers, planning to impress Emmet with her singing. However, events soon take a turn for the worse.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: When Timothy's parents go on a second honeymoon, he plans to hold a party - with the intention of seducing Veronica Straddles.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Northern Soul at the Proms
programme.desc: The roof of the Royal Albert Hall nearly comes off in this special Prom dedicated to northern soul, curated by Wigan's Stuart Maconie and Manchester's Joe Duddell.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who have lived, loved and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, including Cezanne, Monet, Renoir and Matisse.

programme.title: Discovering the Music of Antiquity
programme.desc: The discovery of an ancient music score in the Louvre sets researchers on a mission to recreate the music as it was originally heard by the Greeks 2,400 years ago.

programme.title: Buddha: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes travels to India to investigate the revolutionary ideas of the Buddha.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who have lived, loved and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, including Cezanne, Monet, Renoir and Matisse.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter looks back on three centuries of contact and conflict with Scotland's Roman invaders, a story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary looking into what killed King Tutankhamun, the boy pharaoh. Dallas Campbell reveals new scientific research and carries out unique experiments to get to the truth.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The religious revolution of the 16th century creates radical and surprising works of art. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Amsterdam: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and design of Amsterdam - a city that pioneered much of modern life, from multinational trade to how we design our homes.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary looking into what killed King Tutankhamun, the boy pharaoh. Dallas Campbell reveals new scientific research and carries out unique experiments to get to the truth.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The religious revolution of the 16th century creates radical and surprising works of art. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 4 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Speechless: Storyville
programme.desc: Part two of a film exploring the freedom of speech conversations on US campuses. Contains strong language and scenes which some viewers may find disturbing.

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Nuclear physicist Jim Al-Khalili uncovers the story of the Sellafield nuclear facility.

programme.title: Horizon: Volcanoes of the Solar System
programme.desc: Horizon follows an international team of volcanologists in Iceland as they draw fascinating parallels between the volcanoes on Earth and those elsewhere in the solar system.

programme.title: The Houdini Myth - Timewatch
programme.desc: Film exploring the extraordinary impact of escapologist Harry Houdini in the early 20th century. Featuring restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists.

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Nuclear physicist Jim Al-Khalili uncovers the story of the Sellafield nuclear facility.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 5 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Tensions mount as jealousy, lust and shame combine to test the five flatmates. Contains some strong language and some scenes of a sexual nature.

programme.title: Concorde: A Supersonic Story
programme.desc: The life of the most glamorous plane ever built, as told by the people whose lives she touched. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo.

programme.title: Shark: Beneath the Surface
programme.desc: A look at how an international team of scientists is trying to change people's perceptions of sharks. However, their breakthroughs come at a time when many sharks face extinction.
